name: Pesto Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups Basil leaves, fresh, loosely packed
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter the bottom and sides of a 9-inch springform pan.
2. Combine breadcrumbs and 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese in a small bowl and stir well. Coat the bottom and sides of the buttered pan with the breadcrumb mixture. Chill for 15 minutes.
3. In an electric blender or food processor, combine fresh basil leaves, parsley sprigs, olive oil, salt, and garlic. Process until smooth, scraping sides of the bowl occasionally with a rubber spatula. Transfer to a medium bowl and set aside.
4. Combine ricotta cheese, cream cheese, and 8 ounces Parmesan cheese in an electric blender or food processor. Process until smooth.
5. Add eggs and basil mixture to the cheese mixture. Cover and process until smooth.
6. Pour the basil mixture into the prepared pan. Sprinkle with toasted pine nuts.
7. Place the springform pan in a 15- x 9- x 1-inch jellyroll pan.
8. Bake at 325°F for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
9. Turn off the oven, partially open the oven door, and let the cheesecake cool for 1 hour.
10. Transfer to a wire rack. Remove sides of the springform pan. Serve at room temperature.
